title: Strong US demand boost sentiment in oil market
section_path: Strong US demand boost sentiment in oil market
section_path_text: Strong US demand boost sentiment in oil market
summary:
Main
Crude oil rebounded after Trump pulled back from sanction relief on Iran. Metals were broadly higher amid signs of stronger demand. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-06-26 143022.png | assets/2025-06-26_strong-us-demand-boost-sentiment-in-oil-market_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_0b5a363752a3.png

Market Commentary
Crude oil prices lifted after President Trump said the US would hold a meeting with Iran next week but maintained that he was not giving up his ‘maximum pressure' campaign.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Crude oil rebounded after Trump pulled back from sanction relief on Iran. Metals were broadly higher amid signs of stronger demand.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Crude oil prices lifted after President Trump said the US would hold a meeting with Iran next week but maintained that he was not giving up his ‘maximum pressure' campaign. The comments came as the US seeks to hammer out a new trade framework with China and climb down from the tariff war.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: Crude oil inventories in the US have fallen for five consecutive weeks and are now sitting at their lowest seasonal level since 2014. The apparent strength in demand should provide some support to crude oil prices.
